articles.title: ‘Like mice in a cage’: Inside Europe’s prison overcrowding crisis
articles.summary: In some of Europe’s richest countries, including Belgium, overcrowding is driving a severe deterioration of conditions.

articles.title: Tennis stars rejoice as Serena Williams announces competitive comeback
articles.summary: The 44-year-old will return at the Queen's Club Championships this month after receiving women's doubles wildcard entry.

articles.title: France World Cup 2026 preview: Players to watch, group matches and squad
articles.summary: Departing Deschamps looks to win second title with side led by superstar forwards Kylian Mbappe and Ousmane Dembele.

articles.title: Huge Messi statue taken down in India over safety fears
articles.summary: Huge Messi statue taken down in India over safety fears.

articles.title: Nigerian drifters build track, dream of Formula One
articles.summary: Nigerian drifters build track, dream of Formula One

articles.title: Norway salvages 18th-century shipwreck, recovers porcelain and chandelier
articles.summary: Archaeologists recovered Chinese porcelain and European goods from an 18th-century shipwreck found in southern Norway.

articles.title: Hailstorm hits Denver and causes widespread disruption
articles.summary: A powerful hailstorm swept through the US city of Denver, bringing hailstones reportedly as large as golf balls.

articles.title: Mozambique says 5 citizens killed in ‘xenophobic attacks’ in South Africa
articles.summary: The violence prompted 300 Mozambicans to return home over the weekend.

articles.title: China opens its markets to African exports. Who benefits?
articles.summary: China's tariff-free access for many African products has renewed hopes of industrial growth. But trade remains uneven.

articles.title: Senegal president names government, boycotted by ally-turned-rival
articles.summary: Ousted PM says his party, which holds the parliamentary majority, will not participate in the new government.

articles.title: At least 12 people killed, dozens wounded in Russian attacks on Ukraine
articles.summary: Russia launches 656 drones and 73 missiles at Ukraine overnight, according to Ukraine's air force.

articles.title: South Africa’s World Cup delegation departs for Mexico without coach
articles.summary: Bafana Bafana's departure was delayed due to non-issuance of visas for several players and support staff.

articles.title: Iran war day 95: Trump pushes Lebanon truce after Tehran vows to end talks
articles.summary: Trump says he persuaded Israel and Hezbollah to halt attacks, seeking to prevent a wider war in Lebanon.

articles.title: India’s Zee Entertainment signs World Cup 2026 broadcast deal with FIFA
articles.summary: Zee will broadcast the 2026 and 2030 World Cups and the 2027 Women's World Cup among 39 FIFA tournaments until 2034.

articles.title: Rafael Grossi: the next Iran nuclear deal will look very different
articles.summary: IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi says the 2015 Iran nuclear deal is no longer a workable model.

articles.title: Mexican protesters clash with police ahead of the 2026 World Cup
articles.summary: Teachers marched in Mexico demanding better pay and pensions, warning of further protests ahead of the 2026 World Cup.

articles.title: Google parent Alphabet to sell $80bn in stock to fund AI plans
articles.summary: US tech giant says fundraising drive includes deal to sell $10bn of stock to Berkshire Hathaway.

articles.title: Woman assaulted by Dutch police at asylum centre speaks to Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Malak Mahmoud, the pregnant woman filmed being thrown to the ground by a Dutch police officer has spoken to Al Jazeera.

articles.title: US Defense Department bars journalists from its press office
articles.summary: Media freedom advocates condemn move as latest effort to curtail independent reporting on the US military.

articles.title: President Trump attempts to end hostilities in Lebanon
articles.summary: US President Trump claims a diplomatic breakthrough after speaking with Israeli PM to halt escalation toward Beirut.

articles.title: Celebration, shock and scepticism follow Colombia’s presidential election
articles.summary: Far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella beat left-wing Senator Ivan Cepeda in the first round, upending expectations.

articles.title: Iran war live: Tehran ponders US peace proposal as Israel bombs Lebanon
articles.summary: Iran says it hasn't yet replied to the US's peace plan because of 'distrust' as Israeli strikes on Lebanon continue.

articles.title: Ultra-Orthodox protesters clash with Israeli police over army draft
articles.summary: Police seen dragging ultra-Orthodox protesters from under a bus after they blocked roads in Jerusalem over conscription.

articles.title: Violent protests erupt in Chile over government cuts of social programmes
articles.summary: Labour unions and student groups protested in Chile as President Kast delivered his first State of the Nation.

articles.title: US court upholds injunction against Trump policy banning transgender troops
articles.summary: The decision was split, allowing the Trump administration to continue barring transgender people from enlisting.
